column1: 2004-05-10‚ÄéWe are proud to announce that the top weather forcasting company
column3: has become the official sponsor of the Montana 2004 trek. During the trip you will be able to live vicariously by going to the site and looking up the forcast for where we are.

column1: 2004-06-24‚ÄéWe're putting a lot of photos up on the website under gallery.  Scroll down and look for the pre-wilderness album.  We are not sure if we'll have any contact in the wilderness tomorrow so don't count on hearing from us for awhile.

2004-06-24‚Äéhey everyone! today we woke up early and were on the road at 7:00 to go to yellowstone. we drove for a long time today. we got all the way down to old faithful and it just pained me to not go to my grandparents ranch which is like 2 hours away. we had so much fun today! sarah and i sang a lot on the way there
column2: which people either enjoyed or hated. oh well. we saw bison
column5: and a little black blob which everyone said was a baby black bear (i didn't have my glasses on). we stopped at mcdonalds on the way back and got here at about 9:00pm. i showered and we are going to finish the movie we rented last night (gothica). tomorrow some people (including me!) are going to hike up a mountain which is close to here. it should be great! hope all is well to who ever reads this

column1: 2004-06-22‚ÄéToday was wonderful and so much fun.  It was the first time I was in Wyoming and the first time I peed in the woods this trip.  It was a blast with everyone and seeing old faithful.  There were beautiful mountains and animals.  Also with Mcdonalds and ice cream it doesnt get much better. Tomorrow we are climbing a mountain.  No blisters today so hopefully I wont get them.  Hope everything is well in SC.

column2: we made it! we are having tons of fun. tonight for dinner we had steak and chicken. it was 80 degrees today and we got to go on  a nice walk. tomorrow we will focus on packing for camping and thursday we will visit yellowstone. everyone is so nice and we are having a great time. check out Sarah Tolan's bio for an audio blog from both of us. miss ya'll!

column1: 2004-06-21‚ÄéWell we all made it to Montana and we're safe so you don't have to worry.  Pretty nice here
column2: it rained a little but other than that the weather has been great.

column1: 2004-06-21‚ÄéWell as usual Rachel is going nuts.  She's trying to get the ferret to respond to her high pitch screams.  Yeah....  Anyway people seem to actually want to do this blogging thing so I guess you'll be seeing a lot of the posts around the biographies.  It's all good.  I'll be sending more later.
2004-06-21‚ÄéWell all is well here in Montana.  We just had a thunderstorm run through
column2: but we didn't get all that much rain.  As you've probably heard
column3: this morning I got sick on the airplane.  It's really beautiful out here
column4: and now I'm just trying to get used to the time change.  We had dinner at what would have been my 900.  It was really weird and now we're all dead tired from the long day.  I don't know what we're doing tomorrow
column5: but I guess we'll find out soon!!  ttyl.

column2: I'm cutting this one a little close.  Everything is packed.  But
column3: I needed to set things up so that I can post updates.  So
column4: here is the first one...just hours away from departure for Montana.

column2: I'm not the best at following directions

column1: 2004-06-19‚ÄéI can't believe this actually works. Took me long enough to set it up. Anyway
column2: see everyone bright and early tomorrow!

column1: 2004-06-18‚ÄéWell I've spent a few days trying to get people to sign up to use this tool so they can make posts like this to their biography.  To no avail.  If you bump into any of them that don't have posts like this

column2: i can't believe that we will be able to write stuff in montana and people back at home can read it. i am excited for the trip...only 3 days away!

column1: 2004-06-18‚Äéafter hours of walter's undivided attention (not something everyone can say they have had
column2: but all want) i have finally made myself a blog. i'm still not sure what a blog is because it sounds kind of like an animal but at least i have one now. see you all soon

column1: 2004-06-17‚ÄéI hope this works because it seemed too easy!
2004-06-17‚ÄéWell this is pretty cool that we can post stuff on here
column2: and then people can read it!!  Even cooler about the whole audio thing...although I have yet to try that.  Well
column3: I'm just at home now merely brainstorming about all the stuff that I need to find and obtain for this trip.  It's coming up pretty soon
column4: and it sure is awfully exciting now
column6: and all I have to worry about now is not forgetting anything.  I say overpack.  My luck will probably leave me without any underwear! lol.  Well good luck to everyone else as you go through this packing adventure!

column2: I guess this works.  I'm excited

Walter and I were entertained by Sarah P concerning all of the neat things she purchased to look cool on the trail.  Later this afternoon is the final team meeting to assure that all are ready.  Then Team is free till 5:00 AM Tuesday.  We convene at the airport.  Let the adventure begin. :-)

column1: 2004-06-14‚ÄéHey I finally got these RSS feeds working so we will be able to post stuff during the Montana trip.
2004-06-08‚ÄéWe're almost done packing all the gear for the trip
column3: nobody else has done any work. Hopefully we will have one more preparation day before heading out. I will redesign the template to integrate to the site and I hope to set up audio blogging when I get out.

column1: 2004-07-06‚ÄéAfter eleven straight days in the woods
column2: we have finally emerged for the most part unscathed.  Desperate craving pizza
column3: we have elected to find the nearest joint in Bozeman.  Throughout this long trek many things have come to pass.  In the woods we have sampled squirrel
column4: snake and trout.  We have gone without showers
column5: some elected to use the 5 degree waterfall
column6: but the rest of us held out.  More stories will be posted as time goes on but the most important thing is that all the research got done and our trip was a major success.  We would like to thank everybody that made this possible including our corporate sponsor
